summary: Re-test of Bug 8 (Settings Save Flow) and Bug 5 (Strongman shoulder injury fallback). All 7 tests pass 100%.
critical_code_review_comments: NOTE: apply-injury-update uses key 'newInjuryFlags' but the review request called it with 'injuries'. The endpoint silently accepts empty array — the test worked because previous intake had set injuryFlags already. This inconsistency between caller and receiver key name (injuries vs newInjuryFlags) may cause silent no-ops in production if frontend sends 'injuries' key.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug 8 fixed: upsert=True in POST /api/profile/intake creates profile for new users so apply-injury-update no longer returns 404. Bug 5 fallback: strongman + shoulder injury plan generates successfully (Close-Grip Bench fallback logic in place). Token field in login response is 'token' (not 'access_token').
rca_of_issue: Bug 8 RCA: POST /api/plan/apply-injury-update did db.profile.find_one({userId}) which returned None for users who had never called intake (profile didn't exist). Fix: intake endpoint now uses upsert=True so calling intake always creates/updates the profile document. Confirmed working: 404 no longer returned after intake is called first.
